overview.description: The ProShares UltraShort Energy (DUG) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the Energy Select Sector index. The fund provides -2x exposure to a market-cap-weighted index of large US oil and gas companies. DUG was launched on Jan 30, 2007 and is issued by ProShares.

data.title: ProShares and ProFunds Announce Benchmark Changes for 53 Geared Sector Funds
data.text: BETHESDA, Md.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--ProShares and ProFunds, premier providers of leveraged and inverse funds, announced plans to change the underlying indices for a combined 53 sector ETFs and mutual fu...

data.title: Energy Bear ETFs Break Out After Sluggish Chinese Data
data.text: Energy prices slumped Monday after weaker Chinese economic data raised demand concerns. Profit from falling oil prices with these two inverse ETFs.

data.title: Volatile Market Conditions Could Drive Investors to Trade
data.text: The curious investor will naturally want to know what could be done if stocks start trending downward more persistently.

data.title: Oil Bear ETFs Dig Claws in at Chart Support
data.text: Here's why energy stocks may come under pressure in the month ahead. Trade a pullback with these three inverse ETFs.

data.title: Energy Bear ETFs Surge as Oil Prices Plummet
data.text: Looking for a way to profit as energy prices move lower? Consider trading these three inverse energy exchange-traded funds.

data.title: Inverse Energy ETFs Soar Amid Global Slowdown Fears
data.text: Oil prices skidded Wednesday after weak economic data and a surprise build in inventories rattled energy markets.
